response:
To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $\lambda$ is a scalar variable and $I$ is the identity matrix.

The given matrix is:
$$ A = \begin{pmatrix} 3 & -12 & 13 \\ -6 & 13 & 11 \\ -7 & -6 & -8 \end{pmatrix} $$

The characteristic polynomial is defined as:
$$ p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I) $$

**Step 1: Set up the matrix $A - \lambda I$**
$$ A - \lambda I = \begin{pmatrix} 3 - \lambda & -12 & 13 \\ -6 & 13 - \lambda & 11 \\ -7 & -6 & -8 - \lambda \end{pmatrix} $$

**Step 2: Calculate the determinant**
We expand the determinant along the first row:
$$ \det(A - \lambda I) = (3 - \lambda) \begin{vmatrix} 13 - \lambda & 11 \\ -6 & -8 - \lambda \end{vmatrix} - (-12) \begin{vmatrix} -6 & 11 \\ -7 & -8 - \lambda \end{vmatrix} + 13 \begin{vmatrix} -6 & 13 - \lambda \\ -7 & -6 \end{vmatrix} $$

Let's compute each $2 \times 2$ determinant separately:

1.  **First minor:**
    $$ \begin{vmatrix} 13 - \lambda & 11 \\ -6 & -8 - \lambda \end{vmatrix} = (13 - \lambda)(-8 - \lambda) - (11)(-6) $$
    $$ = -(13 - \lambda)(8 + \lambda) + 66 $$
    $$ = -(104 + 13\lambda - 8\lambda - \lambda^2) + 66 $$
    $$ = -104 - 5\lambda + \lambda^2 + 66 $$
    $$ = \lambda^2 - 5\lambda - 38 $$
